page: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. System memory totals 64.0 GB, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is compatible with Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 31.44 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 13.56 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registers at 105.93 GB/sec. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 404.96 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model averages 2019.86 tokens/sec. Other metrics, including a Passmark CPU score of 26468.23 and Redis SET operations at 21.81 million operations per second, fall within the average performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ns_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of bundled local storage. The absence of local NVMe drives represents a tradeoff, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage for data persistence. Given its network-optimized design, dedicated Intel Xeon processor, and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this server is configured for workloads such as web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale serialization or compression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnv6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression at 31.44 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 13.56 GB/sec. Additionally, it achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 404.96 tokens/sec for small LLM models. Other metrics, such as a Passmark CPU score of 26468.23 and Redis SET operations at 21.81 million operations per second, align with average tiers. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations requiring dedicated compute resources without local storage requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and strong single-threaded compression performance, suited for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory allocation for general-purpose and serialization workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
